title: Equality checks should not be made against numpy.nan
description: Avoid comparing values with numpy.nan. Comparing with numpy.nan will always return False, which could lead to unexpected behavior.

noncompliant.code:
import numpy as np

value = np.nan
if value == np.nan:
    print('Equal to np.nan')
noncompliant.description: Comparison with np.nan will always return False

noncompliant.code:
import numpy
val2 = numpy.nan
if val2 == numpy.nan:
    print('Equal to numpy.nan')
noncompliant.description: Comparison with numpy.nan will always return False

compliant.code:
import numpy as np

value = np.nan
if np.isnan(value):
    print('Nan')
compliant.description: Use numpy.isnan() to check for numpy.nan

why_is_this_an_issue: Comparing with numpy.nan will always return False, which could lead to unexpected behavior.
how_to_fix: Use the numpy.isnan() function to check for napym.nan.
